interpretation: The cluster-size-distribution (Fisher) exponent tau_F is position-independent and robust to road-density inhomogeneity. The square-lattice calibration recovers the exact 2D value 2.055; real road graphs at their observed thresholds give tau_F close to the 2D value and well below the mean-field/random-graph value 2.5 obtained for a degree-matched configuration model at its CEBH threshold. This supports a near-two-dimensional finite-size critical-class interpretation for the tested road windows rather than a mean-field random-graph reading. The accessible 10-km windows limit precision, so this is supporting evidence, not a high-precision exponent measurement or a proof of universality for all road systems.
